title_en: The 22 Very Online Upstarts Changing the Face of Politics
title_zh: 改变政治面貌的22位“高度在线”新势力
rubric_en: Introducing WIRED’s 2025 Political Power Users—the creators, podcasters, and pundits who will blow up the next electoral era.
rubric_zh: 介绍《WIRED》评选的2025年政治“意见领袖”——这些内容创作者、播客人和评论员，将在下一轮选举时代掀起风暴。

paragraphs.en: Donald Trump’s second term has ushered in a new era in American politics. It’s brasher, crueler, more direct, more super online, and certainly more dystopian.
paragraphs.zh: 唐纳德·特朗普的第二任期开启了美国政治的新纪元。它更粗放、更冷酷、更直接，也更“超在线”，而且无疑更像反乌托邦。

paragraphs.en: Democrats and sometimes even Republicans have struggled to compete with Trump’s monopoly on the attention market. But the leaders of both parties are only mortal—yes, even Trump—and they’re not going to be around forever.
paragraphs.zh: 无论是民主党人，还是有时的共和党人，都很难与特朗普在公众注意力市场上的垄断竞争。但双方党派的领导者终究都是凡人——是的，连特朗普也包括在内——而且他们都不会永远在场。

paragraphs.en: A new generation of political talent is muscling its way onto the scene, armed with new ways of connecting with the masses and original visions of the country they want to live in. Here are the candidates, the influencers, and the insiders on both the left and the right that we think you’ll keep hearing from, and why. You might just remember when you spotted a future president in this edition of WIRED.
paragraphs.zh: 一代新的政治新人正在凭借与大众连接的新方式，以及对自己想要的国家生活愿景的原创想法，逐步闯入舞台。以下是我们认为你会继续听到的、来自左右两派的候选人、意见影响者和幕僚，以及原因。也许在本期《WIRED》中，你就会认出一位未来总统。

paragraphs.en: After the shock of the 2024 election loss, some Democrats became convinced of the need for a “liberal Joe Rogan”—someone to appeal to the podcast-bro demographic that helped put Trump back in the White House. But there are already Democrats and progressives who know how to mobilize an online audience—and think they know where the party’s communications have gone wrong.
paragraphs.zh: 在经历2024年选举失利的冲击后，一些民主党人确信需要一位“自由派乔·罗根”——也就是一位能够吸引把特朗普重新送回白宫的播客文化男性听众群体的人物。但其实，已经有不少民主党人和进步派懂得如何动员线上受众，而且认为他们也知道党内的传播哪里出了问题。

paragraphs.en: If you were caught up in the avalanche of content coming from the New York City mayoral primary race, you likely saw a video by Melted Solids, which worked with Zohran Mamdani early on in his campaign. Cofounders Anthony DiMieri and Debbie Saslaw come from advertising and content production backgrounds, not politics. They bring a documentary-style approach focused on platforming regular people. “Listening, not lecturing,” as Saslaw describes it. One of their most viral collaborations with Mamdani is a video of the candidate interviewing Trump voters (and nonvoters) in Queens and the Bronx.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你关注了纽约市市长初选中铺天盖地而来的内容，你很可能见过 Melted Solids 制作的一段视频。该团队在 Zohran Mamdani 竞选初期就与他合作。联合创始人 Anthony DiMieri 和 Debbie Saslaw 的背景是广告和内容制作，而不是政治，他们采用的是纪录片式方法，重点让普通人有发言平台。正如 Saslaw 所说，这叫“倾听，而不是说教”。他们与 Mamdani 合作最具病毒传播性的作品之一，是一段他在皇后区和布朗克斯采访特朗普选民（以及不投票者）的视频。

paragraphs.en: But not everyone can nail the essence of a Melted Solids video, or even understand what makes them special. In Andrew Cuomo’s unsuccessful attempt at mimicking Mamdani, he’s seen hand-shaking and back-slapping potential voters, but their voices remain unheard. You’re likely to keep seeing DiMieri and Saslaw’s influence on political messaging, though, thanks to Mamdani’s upset victory in June.
paragraphs.zh: 但并不是每个人都能把握住 Melted Solids 视频的精髓，甚至都无法理解它为何特别。阿伦·库奥莫（Andrew Cuomo）在模仿 Mamdani 的失败尝试中，只是和潜在选民握手拍肩，但他们的声音依旧没有被听见。由于 Mamdani 在六月意外获胜，你大概会继续在政治宣传话语中看到 DiMieri 和 Saslaw 的影响力。

paragraphs.en: “Before I am an elected official, before I am a son, before I am a brother, I am a shitposter, and I have always been,” says Ossé. “If there’s another language that I speak, it is the internet.” The 27-year-old City Council member is the creator of several videos with millions of views on Instagram, including his series “Why Shit Not Working?” that breaks down the most intransigent elements of New York City’s dysfunction.
paragraphs.zh: “在我当选前，在我成为儿子前，在我成为兄弟前，我就是个垃圾贴文制造者，而且我一直如此，”Ossé 说。“如果说我会说另一种语言，那就是网络语言。”这位27岁的市议员在 Instagram 上创作了多支视频，累计获得数百万次观看，其中包括系列节目《Why Shit Not Working?》（“为什么一切这么烂？”），拆解了纽约市最难对付的各种体制性失灵问题。

paragraphs.en: Ossé frequently uses his online presence to galvanize public opinion and even once to pass policy. With one social media call in 2023, he got more than 1,000 people to attend a board meeting on rent guidelines to voice concern about a double-digit percent proposed increase on rent-stabilized apartments. Now other politicians are taking cues from his success, including Mamdani—Ossé says he had to remind the mayoral candidate to post his campaign launch video on TikTok.
paragraphs.zh: Ossé 经常利用自己的网络影响力来推动舆论，甚至曾经一次用社媒呼吁就推动过政策。2023 年，在他的一次社交媒体号召下，超过 1,000 人前来参加租金指导委员会会议，表达对对受保护租金公寓拟议双位数涨幅的担忧。现在，其他政治人物也在借鉴他的成功经验，包括 Mamdani——Ossé 甚至说自己得提醒这位市长候选人在 TikTok 上发布其竞选启动视频。

paragraphs.en: Foxx’s star rose practically overnight in 2017, when the then-16-year-old’s exchange with US senator Jeff Flake over his vote to restrict funding to Planned Parenthood went viral. The activist and content creator built a career on that moment, working as a digital strategist for the Kamala Harris 2020 campaign and then appearing as a speaker at the 2024 Democratic National Convention.
paragraphs.zh: 2017年，Foxx 的名声几乎一夜之间飙升，当时她只有16岁，与美国参议员杰夫·弗莱克就其限制给“计划生育联盟（Planned Parenthood）”拨款的表决发生的争执迅速在网络上流传开来。这位活动人士兼内容创作者正是凭借这场视频火爆开启了自己的职业道路，随后在卡玛拉·哈里斯2020年竞选中担任数字策略人员，并于2024年在民主党全国代表大会上登上讲台发言。

paragraphs.en: This year, the 25-year-old launched her own campaign in a special election for the late Raúl Grijalva’s Arizona congressional seat. In a July TikTok video that she filmed with family members, she reminisced about filing campaign paperwork online: alone, in her bedroom, “with no staff, no donor list.”
paragraphs.zh: 今年，这位25岁的她在一场为已故Raúl·格里哈尔瓦的亚利桑那州国会席位举行的补选中发起了自己的竞选。她在7月发布的一段 TikTok 视频中与家人一起回忆起自己的竞选申报流程：她独自在卧室里在线办理材料，“没有工作人员，也没有捐赠人名单”。

paragraphs.en: The rest of her social media is just as earnest and direct, featuring front-facing videos that ground her progressive policies in her life experience: Foxx was raised by a single mom, experienced homelessness, and relied on federally funded programs like Section 8 and Title X.
paragraphs.zh: 她的其他社交媒体内容同样真诚而直接，常以正面出镜视频为主，将她的进步主张与个人经历联系起来：Foxx 是单亲家庭长大的，曾经历过无家可归，并长期依赖联邦资助项目，如“第八条款”住房补贴和 Title X 计划生育相关项目。

paragraphs.en: While Foxx ultimately lost the primary to Adelita Grijalva, Raúl’s daughter, her digital strategy generated notable momentum in the last few weeks of her campaign. She hit 300,000 TikTok followers and raised hundreds of thousands of dollars in small donations. Despite her loss, Foxx caught the attention of younger progressives in the House of Representatives, who say they are excited about her future in politics.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管 Foxx 最终在初选中输给了 Adelita Grijalva（Raúl 的女儿），但她的数字化竞选策略在活动最后几周仍带来了显著的势头。她在 TikTok 上吸引了30万粉丝，并通过小额捐款筹得数十万美元。尽管落选，Foxx 仍引起了众多众议院年轻进步派议员的关注，这些议员表示对她在政治道路上的前景感到兴奋。

paragraphs.en: Rutinel, 30, may have invented a new way to campaign online: via heartfelt Instagram photo montages set to pop music, sometimes sung by the man himself. Now he’s deploying these videos in the race to unseat Republican representative Gabe Evans in Colorado’s Eighth District, which is projected to be one of the midterms’ most competitive races.
paragraphs.zh: 30岁的Rutinel 也许发明了一种全新的线上竞选方式：用充满真情实感的 Instagram 照片拼贴视频，再配上流行音乐，有时还由他本人亲自演唱。现在，他正在这场竞选中投放这些视频，目标是击败科罗拉多州第八选区的共和党众议员Gabe Evans，这场选举已被预测将成为今年中期选举中最激烈的角逐之一。

paragraphs.en: Rutinel’s posts are often deliberately hammy. (He once did a Christopher Walken impression to announce a motion in the Colorado state legislature.) They also consistently deliver his campaign message: that he’s a champion for working people because of his own working-class upbringing. (Rutinel did not respond to WIRED’s request for an interview.)
paragraphs.zh: Rutinel 的发帖常常故意卖弄表演感。（他曾模仿 Christopher Walken 的语气和语调，在科罗拉多州立法机构里宣布一项动议。）这些帖子也始终清晰地传递出他的竞选主张：凭借自己出身工人阶层的经历，他是为劳动者发声的斗士。（WIRED 曾向 Rutinel 发出采访请求，但他未予回应。）

paragraphs.en: In the first six months of 2025, Rutinel raised more than $1.6 million, about half of which came from small-dollar donations. In the crowded field of Democratic candidates opposing Evans, he is the only one who has kept up with the Republican’s fundraising. As the race gains national attention, Rutinel’s distinct online presence might earn some copycats in years to come.
paragraphs.zh: 截至2025年上半年，Rutinel 已筹得超过160万美元，其中大约一半来自小额捐款。在反对 Evans 的拥挤民主党人选阵容中，他是唯一一个能够持续追平共和党对手筹款速度的人。随着这场竞选受到全国关注，Rutinel 的独特线上风格可能会在未来几年催生不少跟风者。

paragraphs.en: Over on the right, some of the youngest members of Trumpworld have iterated on The Donald’s playbook, mixing traditional and new media to reach the base and capture everyone else’s attention.
paragraphs.zh: 在右翼阵营中，一些最年轻的“特朗普圈”人物也在反复改写“唐纳德”的玩法，将传统媒体与新媒体混合运用，既牢牢吸引核心支持者，也抢占更广泛公众的注意。

paragraphs.en: The 36-year-old Air Force veteran is the apotheosis of the GOP’s digital strategy: the nation’s first influencer turned right-wing member of Congress. Paulina Luna got there with a canny sense for what works online, and what doesn’t.
paragraphs.zh: 这位 36 岁的空军退伍军人，是共和党数字化战略的集大成者：她是美国第一位从“网红”身份走入国会的右翼议员。保利娜·卢娜（Paulina Luna）正是凭借对哪些内容在线上有效、哪些无效的敏锐判断走到今天的。

paragraphs.en: “If you want to be effective in the future of politics, especially in the next presidential election or the next statewide races, you have to have a presence on social media,” she told WIRED. “But it can’t be a shit presence. It has to be a legit presence, because people can see right through it.” Paulina Luna may have clips of her appearances on Fox News to share, but she says followers would much rather see her extemporize directly to her iPhone for 45 seconds on whatever subject, from UFOs to the culture wars. The House GOP has taken notice of Paulina Luna’s success: Her communications director was invited to speak to members about how to use vertical video.
paragraphs.zh: 卢娜对 WIRED 表示：“如果你想在未来的政治舞台上发挥影响力，尤其是在下一次总统选举或下一轮全州范围选举中，你必须在社交媒体上有存在感。’但不能是‘烂尾’的存在感，而必须是真正有价值的存在感，因为人们一眼就能看穿。”卢娜表示，虽然她可以分享自己在福克斯新闻上的出镜片段，但粉丝更愿意看到她用 iPhone 即兴聊 45 秒，谈论从不明飞行物到文化战争等各种议题。美国众议院共和党也已注意到卢娜的成功：她的通讯主任受邀向成员们介绍如何使用竖屏短视频。

paragraphs.en: After building a formidable following with her Daily Wire show The Comments Section, 23-year-old Cooper struck out on her own earlier this year. Within six months of launching on YouTube, in early 2025, The Brett Cooper Show accrued more than 1.5 million subscribers, racking up hundreds of thousands of views each episode.
paragraphs.zh: 在《The Comments Section》播客/节目为她打下坚实受众基础后，23 岁的科普特（Cooper）今年早些时候独立创业。2025 年初，科普特在 YouTube 开播后仅六个月，《The Brett Cooper Show》便吸引了超过 150 万订阅者，每集都积累了数十万次观看。

paragraphs.en: Cooper doesn’t stray far from the politics-meets-pop-culture formula that made her Daily Wire show a success, but she has ditched explicitly partisan rhetoric for more coded cultural commentary. She picks topics that capture the attention of young women, offering a light-touch conservative critique of, say, Blake Lively’s lawsuit against Justin Baldoni.
paragraphs.zh: 科普特并未偏离让《The Comments Section》成功的“政治与流行文化结合”公式，但她已经放弃了公开、直接的党派口号式说法，转向更隐晦的文化评论。她会挑选吸引年轻女性注意的话题，以轻描淡写的保守主义视角切入，比如评论布莱克·莱弗莉（Blake Lively）对贾斯汀·巴尔多尼（Justin Baldoni）的诉讼。

paragraphs.en: “I never want my content to be a time suck,” Cooper says. “I never want it to be like mindless fluff.”
paragraphs.zh: “我从不想让自己的内容变成时间黑洞，”Cooper 说，“我也不想让它变成无意义的空洞废话。”

paragraphs.en: The 24-year-old White House correspondent for Real America’s Voice knows how to make an entrance. She’s part of the West Wing’s new, young, and vocally right-wing press corps, and her outfits alone generated entire news cycles early in the second Trump administration—mostly thanks to the Daily Mail compiling social media scoldings over her more casual attire. Winters leaned into it, dunking on them and using the coverage to keep building her brand.
paragraphs.zh: 《Real America’s Voice》的这位 24 岁白宫记者很懂得如何造势。她是白宫新一代且喧闹激烈的右翼记者团成员之一，仅凭穿着，在特朗普第二任期初期就引发了完整的新闻循环，主要是因为《Daily Mail》将社交媒体上对她更休闲穿搭的吐槽集中整理并发布。Winters 顺势利用了这些批评，反击它们，并借助相关报道持续打造自己的个人品牌。

paragraphs.en: Winters, who abstains from both alcohol and tap water as part of her wellness regimen, tells WIRED that she thinks the MAHA movement is the new “gateway drug” for the GOP.
paragraphs.zh: 在健康计划中，Winters 同时戒酒且不喝自来水。她告诉 WIRED，她认为 MAHA 运动是共和党新的“入门试剂”。

paragraphs.en: Winters also cohosts War Room on Real America’s Voice with Steve Bannon, her mentor, and has a direct line to the MAGA base heading into 2028.
paragraphs.zh: Winters 还与她的导师、Steve Bannon 一起在《Real America’s Voice》主持《War Room》，并在迈向 2028 年选举之前，与 MAGA 基层支持者保持了直接联系。

paragraphs.en: The coalition that helped elect Barack Obama twice—college-educated, young, Black, and Latino voters—cracked in 2016 and fractured completely in 2024. Now a new slate of candidates on the right are trying to prove that it’s not just Trump who can broaden the GOP’s base.
paragraphs.zh: 曾帮助巴拉克·奥巴马赢得两次总统大选的联盟——受过大学教育的、年轻的、黑人和拉丁裔选民——在2016年出现裂痕，并在2024年彻底解体。如今，一批新的右翼候选人正在努力证明，扩大共和党基础并不只是特朗普的专属能力。

paragraphs.en: The 39 year-old Flores became the first Mexican-born congresswoman in 2022, when she flipped a Texas House seat—the 34th District—in a special election. After losing the next two races for that district, she’s now going to try again, thanks to redistricting that has made the area much redder.
paragraphs.zh: 这位39岁女性在2022年成为第一位墨西哥出生的国会议员，当时她在一次特别选举中赢下了德克萨斯州第34选区的国会席位。后来她在接下来该选区的两场竞选中失利，但由于重新划区使该地区更加偏红，她现在将再次参选。

paragraphs.en: Flores aims her candidacy directly at the idea of demographic destiny. “As I grew up, I began to question why so many Hispanics consider themselves Democrats, since the majority of us have been raised with strong conservative values,” she says in one of her earliest campaign videos. “The Hispanic community is pro-God, pro-life, pro-family, pro-America.” (Flores did not respond to WIRED’s request for an interview.)
paragraphs.zh: 弗洛雷斯把自己的参选目标直接对准了“人口结构命运”这一观念。“我长大后开始质疑，为什么那么多拉美裔会把自己认作民主党人，因为我们大多数人是在非常保守的价值观中长大的，”她在最早的一段竞选视频中说。“拉美裔社群是亲上帝的、反堕胎的、亲家庭的、亲美国的。”（WIRED向弗洛雷斯提出采访请求，但她未予回应。）

paragraphs.en: The Navy veteran is running to flip Michigan’s Eighth District as a “proud Muslim” who supports Israel and the America First agenda. If he wins, Hassan would represent his native Flint, the seat of a yearslong water crisis that has shaken residents’ trust in government.
paragraphs.zh: 这位海军退伍军人正以一名“自豪的穆斯林”身份参选，要在密歇根州第8选区完成“反向攻势”，并支持以色列与“美国优先”议程。如果他赢了，哈桑将代表自己的故乡弗林特——这个长期水危机让居民对政府信任受到冲击的地方。

paragraphs.en: Today, the district leans left. But Genesee County, where Flint sits, shifted more than 5 percentage points for Trump in 2024. Hassan sees an opportunity for change.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，这个选区整体偏向左翼。但弗林特所在的吉尼斯县在2024年对特朗普的支持度却增长了超过5个百分点。哈桑看到了一个改变现状的机会。

paragraphs.en: “Where I’m from, we’re raised to think Democrats are the good guys. We’ve been held culturally hostage for over half a century,” the 39 year-old says in a campaign video. “But look around. Flint is what happens when your vote is taken for granted.” (Hassan’s campaign also declined to be interviewed for this piece.)
paragraphs.zh: “在我成长的地方，我们从小被教育民主党人是好人。我们在文化上已经被束缚了半个多世纪。”这位39岁的人在一段竞选视频中说。“但你们看看身边的一切。弗林特就是当你的选票被视为理所当然时会发生的后果。”（哈桑的竞选团队也拒绝了本篇采访。）

paragraphs.en: A growing group of Democratic politicians at different levels of government have challenged their party’s fecklessness with plans to address intractable problems like public safety and the cost of living. The most ambitious among them want to completely remake Congress.
paragraphs.zh: 一批不断壮大的民主党政治人物在不同级别的政府中，正在用解决公共安全和生活成本等顽固难题的方案，挑战本党长期失灵的局面。其中最有野心的人，想要彻底重塑美国国会。

paragraphs.en: The 39-year-old Chakrabarti cofounded Brand New Congress—which was aligned with the so-called Justice Democrats, the group that recruited Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez. He ran AOC’s campaign before becoming her chief of staff in Washington, where he helped write the Green New Deal.
paragraphs.zh: 这位39岁的查克拉巴蒂共同创办了“全新国会”（Brand New Congress）——这个组织与被称为“正义民主党人”的团体结盟，该团体曾招募阿历山德里亚·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯（AOC）。他曾在奥卡西奥-科尔特斯的竞选活动中担任主力，之后成为她在华盛顿的首席幕僚长，并帮助起草了《绿色新政》。

paragraphs.en: Chakrabarti says he felt compelled to run for Nancy Pelosi’s seat in Congress after he watched the House Democratic leader and other Democrats in the wake of Trump’s 2024 win, “helpless to really change anything.” He has a detailed set of proposals for California’s Eleventh District, including the extensive Mission for America plan to transform the US economy through massive mobilization and investment in green technology. The country needs ambition on that scale, he says, to kick it out of its current state of disorder. Chakrabarti hopes to inspire others to run for Congress and remake the Democratic Party.
paragraphs.zh: 查克拉巴蒂表示，在目睹特朗普在2024年获胜后，众议院民主党领袖南希·佩洛西以及其他民主党人的无力，“几乎无法真正改变任何事”，他便感到自己有必要竞选佩洛西的国会席位。他为加利福尼亚第11选区制定了详细方案，其中包括通过大规模动员和对绿色技术投入来重塑美国经济的“美国任务（Mission for America）”计划。他说，国家需要如此规模的雄心，才能摆脱当前的紊乱状态。查克拉巴蒂希望能够激励更多人参选国会，重塑民主党。

paragraphs.en: “It’s got to be whole new people with new ideas, who are clear on what they stand for and what they’re fighting for, taking over the party,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: “必须是全新的政治人物和新想法的力量来接管这个党，他们要在立场和斗争方向上都清楚，知道自己在争取什么，”他表示。

paragraphs.en: Fateh has been called the “Mamdani of Minneapolis,” nominally because he is also a state legislator challenging an incumbent mayor with a campaign focused on affordability and standing up to Trump. But the comparison has been wielded in transparently racist ways, too: before he died, Charlie Kirk began fearmongering over the 35-year-old’s Islamic faith.
paragraphs.zh: 法特（Fateh）曾被称为“明尼阿波利斯的Mamdani”。这主要是因为他既是州立法委员，又以关注可负担性并且对抗特朗普为核心展开竞选，挑战现任市长。不过，这个比较也曾被明显地带上种族主义色彩：在他去世前，查理·柯克（Charlie Kirk）就开始对这位35岁的候选人的伊斯兰信仰进行煽动式恐惧宣传。

paragraphs.en: Fateh says the current Democratic mayor of Minneapolis, Jacob Frey, has failed to end homelessness and transform public safety in a city still grappling with the murder of George Floyd. His primary focus is on the city’s cost of living, and he’s bringing proposals to raise the minimum wage, spur housing development, and implement rent stabilization. But a trail of dueling op-eds in the Minneapolis Star Tribune signals that some consider this race a battle between the democratic socialist wing of the party and the moderate progressives.
paragraphs.zh: 法特称，明尼阿波利斯现任民主党市长雅各布·弗雷（Jacob Frey）未能结束无家可归问题，也未能改善一个仍在应对乔治·弗洛伊德谋杀阴影的城市公共安全。他的核心议题是降低城市生活成本，并正在提出提高最低工资、推动住房建设和实施租金稳定政策的方案。但明尼阿波利斯《星报》（Minneapolis Star Tribune）上一连串交锋社论显示，部分人认为这场竞选是党内民主社会主义阵营与温和进步派之间的较量。

paragraphs.en: “Who does our city trust to lead in the time of crisis?” Fateh says. “Do we want leadership that’s rooted in justice and compassion, or do we want more performative politics?”
paragraphs.zh: “在危机时刻，我们把这座城市交给谁来领导？”Fateh 说。“我们想要一种植根于公正与同情的领导，还是想要更多做作的表演式政治？”

paragraphs.en: While Trump and the right obsess over imagined crime waves in Blue State cities, they have conveniently ignored the real turnaround Scott has overseen in Baltimore. The 41-year-old was elected in 2020 after a handful of corruption scandals involving previous Democratic mayors. Five years and one reelection later, the city has seen homicides drop to their lowest level in a decade, thanks in part to his detailed plan to reduce gun violence.
paragraphs.zh: 而当特朗普及其右翼继续把目光紧盯在蓝州城市中想象中的犯罪浪潮时，他们却顺势忽视了 Scott 在巴尔的摩真正推动的转变。2020 年，这位 41 岁的市长在前任民主党市长频发腐败丑闻后当选。五年后并连任一次，如今这座城市的谋杀案已降至十年来的最低水平，这在一定程度上得益于他的一套细致的降枪支暴力方案。

paragraphs.en: It used to be a predictable arc for those who wanted to climb the Democratic Party ranks: Spend a few terms on the back benches of Congress or in a state legislature, gain some seniority, get a leadership role on a good committee, and then, maybe, you could say you had arrived.
paragraphs.zh: 过去，若想在民主党体系内上位，这是条很可预测的路径：先在国会后排议员席或州议会任职几届，积累资历，在一个好委员会里拿到领导职位，然后也许你就能说自己“有份量”了。

paragraphs.en: Now a whole host of Democrats who grew up online have been able to sustain viral fame and turn it into fundraising and organizing prowess.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，大量成长于网络时代的民主党人已经能够维持病毒式的知名度，并把它转化为募款与组织动员能力。

paragraphs.en: The 39-year-old state senator became a minor celebrity among politics nerds in 2022. After a Republican colleague delivered an invocation on the state senate floor, claiming “children are under attack,” McMorrow walked out of the chamber. Several days later, that same politician sent out a fundraising email with the baseless allegation that McMorrow wants to “groom and sexualize kindergartners.” In a speech addressing the email, McMorrow said, “I am the biggest threat to your hollow, hateful scheme … Hate will only win if people like me stand by and let it happen.” That video, of course, went viral.
paragraphs.zh: 这位 39 岁的州参议员在 2022 年间曾在政治爱好者中小有名气。一次，一位共和党同僚在州参议院议场发表祈祷词，声称“孩子正受到攻击”，麦克默罗（McMorrow）因此走出了会议厅。几天后，这位同僚发出一封筹款邮件，毫无依据地指控麦克默罗想“诱导并性化幼儿园孩子”。在一场回应这封邮件的讲话中，麦克默罗说：“我是对你们那套空洞而充满仇恨的阴谋的最大威胁……仇恨只会赢利，因为像我这样的人袖手旁观并任其发生。”当然，这段视频随后在网络上疯传。

paragraphs.en: McMorrow could have been a flash in the pan, but she managed to make that moment last, becoming a highly sought-after campaign surrogate and featured speaker at fundraising events around the country. Now she’s running for US Senate in one of the most competitive races of the 2025 midterms. One of her influences, McMorrow tells WIRED, came from working at Gawker.
paragraphs.zh: 麦克默罗本可以只是昙花一现，但她让那一刻持续发光，成为炙手可热的竞选代言人，并在全国各地的筹款活动中担任主讲嘉宾。如今，她正竞逐美国参议院，参选的是 2025 年中期选举中最具竞争性的 races 之一。麦克默罗告诉 WIRED，她的一位影响者来自她在 Gawker 的工作经历。

paragraphs.en: “You can talk about policy, you can talk about powerful people, you can talk about something fun, and you can mix and match these things,” she says. “That’s what I do in my digital presence sometimes: I’m talking about being a mom, and a funny thing my daughter said on Instagram last night.”
paragraphs.zh: “你可以谈政策，可以谈有影响力的人物，也可以谈一些轻松有趣的事，还可以把这些内容混合在一起，”她说，“这也是我有时在数字内容里做的事情：我会谈到自己做母亲的经历，以及我女儿昨晚在 Instagram 上说的一句有趣的话。”

paragraphs.en: Wahls had his own stand-and-deliver moment back in 2011, when the then-19-year-old spoke at a public forum about how, while he was “raised by two women,” his family wasn’t so different from other Iowans. After his remarks went viral, Wahls built a community around children of same-sex parents and won a state senate seat in 2019.
paragraphs.zh: 2011 年，瓦尔斯（Wahls）也有过自己的“站出来就引爆舆论”的时刻，当时还只有 19 岁的他在一个公开论坛上发言，说自己“在两个女性的抚养下长大”，并强调他的家庭和其他爱荷华州人并无太大不同。在他的言论走红后，瓦尔斯围绕同性伴侣家庭的子女建立了一个社群，并在 2019 年赢得了一席州参议员席位。

paragraphs.en: “I had no idea the impact that speech would have on my life,” Wahls, now 34, tells WIRED. But it revealed something to him. Authenticity has a way of cutting through the noise. Now he’s running for Joni Ernst's US Senate seat.
paragraphs.zh: “我完全没想到那次演讲会对我人生造成怎样的影响，”现年34岁的Wahls告诉《WIRED》。但那次演讲确实让他发现了某种东西——真实感总有一种穿透噪音、直达人心的力量。如今，他正在竞逐乔尼·恩斯特的美国参议院席位。

paragraphs.en: Wahls is also attuned to what’s at the core of younger voters’ beef with the US economic and political system. “It feels like the ladder is being pulled up,” the Democrat says. “So many people can’t become homeowners, can’t start a career, can’t start a family … those issues affect people in the most personal and intimate parts of their lives.” With his focus on economic populism, including campaign promises to break up monopolies and raise the minimum wage, Wahls is charting a potential course for young Democratic hopefuls in redder states.
paragraphs.zh: Wahls也抓住了年轻选民与美国经济和政治体系矛盾的核心。那位民主党人说：“总觉得向上爬的那架梯子正在被人往上拔。” “太多人买不起房、难以开始职业生涯、也很难成家……这些问题直接触及他们生活中最个人、最私密的部分。”在其以经济民粹主义为核心、包括承诺打破垄断和提高最低工资等主张的竞选纲领下，Wahls正在为红色州份里可能崛起的年轻民主党候选人描绘一条潜在道路。

paragraphs.en: The Lone Star State sits at the crossroads of America’s changing political landscape. Over the past few years, a new generation of unapologetically progressive Democrats have challenged the GOP’s grasp on politics, threatening to shift the state blue. That fight exploded in spectacular fashion at the Texas statehouse this summer when Republicans tried to force a vote gerrymandering themselves more deep-red districts.
paragraphs.zh: 德州这块“孤星之州”正处在美国政治格局变化的十字路口。过去几年里，一批毫不掩饰自己进步立场的年轻民主党人开始挑战共和党在政治上的统治，试图推动州内向蓝州转变。这场拉锯在今年夏天于德州州议会爆发到最引人注目的阶段，当时共和党试图强推一项可被视作自我“捆绑”成更红色选区的界线重划法案。

paragraphs.en: Martin started out in politics as a voting-rights activist at the University of Houston, but the 27-year-old had his breakout moment this year as Republicans attempted to push through their controversial gerrymandering effort. In an act of civil disobedience that went viral online, Martin was arrested and forced out of a public hearing. (The charges were later dropped.)
paragraphs.zh: Martin在休斯敦大学时期最初是投票权倡导者，后来才逐步走进政治，但这位27岁人，今年迎来真正出圈时刻，要在共和党推动这项有争议的界线重划提案时。通过一次在网上广泛传播的“非暴力不合作”抗议行动，Martin被逮捕并被赶出了一场公开听证会。（相关指控后来被撤销。）

paragraphs.en: “I do see a blue Texas on the horizon,” says Martin, who is running in a special election for the 18th District this November. But to get there, he says, Democrats need to fight fire with fire. That means more protesting and using similarly controversial tactics, like breaking legislative quorum. “We’ve tried to hold ourselves to a different moral standard,” Martin says. “On the other side, you have Republicans that are going for a jugular.”
paragraphs.zh: “我确实看到地平线上有一片蓝色的德州，”正在今年11月参与第18选区特别选举的马丁说。不过，要达到这一点，他认为民主党人需要以其人之道还治其人之身。这就意味着更激烈的抗议，并采用同样有争议的策略，比如人为使立法机构达不到法定人数而中断表决。“我们一直试图保持更高的道德标准，”马丁说。“而在对面，你会看到共和党人是直接瞄准要害的。”

paragraphs.en: Texas Democrats fleeing the state to avoid a vote on the GOP’s gerrymandering bill is a good start, according to Martin, but the real challenge will be whether they dare to do more.
paragraphs.zh: 德州民主党人为了避免对共和党红istricting（重新划区）法案进行表决而出州避会，按照马丁的说法是一个好的开端，但真正的挑战在于，他们是否敢走得更远。

paragraphs.en: The 36-year-old former public school teacher went viral with a speech opposing a bill that would require public schools to display the Ten Commandments in classrooms. The clip landed him on Joe Rogan’s podcast earlier this summer. The interview, which has nearly a million views on YouTube, shot Talarico to political stardom, positioning him as one of the most promising Texas progressives in recent years.
paragraphs.zh: 这位36岁的前公立学校教师因为反对一项要求公立学校在教室内张贴《十诫》的法案而发表演讲，演讲视频一度走红。该片段使他在今年夏天登上了乔·罗根的播客节目。那段采访在YouTube上已接近100万次观看，让塔拉里科直接跃升为政治明星，也使他成为近年来德州最有前景的进步派人物之一。

paragraphs.en: “People are fascinated by Texas, always have been,” Talarico says. “They are excited by our state. They’re terrified by our state. Texas is America on steroids.”
paragraphs.zh: “人们对德州一直很着迷，”塔拉里科说。“他们对我们的州充满期待，也被我们的州震撼。德州像是‘加了强心剂’的美国。”

paragraphs.en: Often, the most powerful movers and shakers in Washington, DC, are the faces you see the least (well, anywhere other than their own social media feeds). They’re on the phone connecting their politician bosses with podcasters or throwing parties establishing a new political scene.
paragraphs.zh: 在华盛顿特区，最有力量、最能推动局势的人物，往往是你最少见到的那些面孔（除了出现在他们自己的社交媒体动态里）。他们一边给政客上级打电话，把政客与播客主撮合在一起，一边举办聚会，搭建一种全新的政治场景。

paragraphs.en: As senior media adviser to Trump’s reelection campaign, the 28 year-old Bruesewitz was the mastermind behind the president’s podcast strategy. By placing Trump on shows like Theo Von’s This Past Weekend and the NELK Boys’ Full Send Podcast, Bruesewitz helped young, predominantly male audiences see the president in a new light.
paragraphs.zh: 作为特朗普连任竞选的高级媒体顾问，28 岁的布鲁伊威茨（Bruesewitz）是总统播客策略的幕后策划者。通过让特朗普上“泰奥·冯（Theo Von）的《This Past Weekend》”以及 NELK Boys 的“Full Send Podcast”等节目，布鲁伊威茨帮助年轻、以男性为主的受众以一种全新的方式认识特朗普。

paragraphs.en: “Whether you’re a comedian making people laugh, or you’re a frat bro chugging beer and body-slamming a ping-pong table,” Bruesewitz says, “you have to lean into your authenticity.” Now, Bruesewitz is leading digital messaging at Never Surrender, Trump’s leadership PAC, setting the tone for how MAGA Republicans communicate online.
paragraphs.zh: “不管你是个让人发笑的喜剧演员，还是一个一口气喝下啤酒、把乒乓球台压在身上的兄弟会男孩，”布鲁伊威茨说，“你都得坚持自己的真实感。” 现在，布鲁伊威茨正在特朗普领导 PAC“Never Surrender”负责数字传播，他正在为 MAGA 共和党人在网上的沟通方式定下调性。

paragraphs.en: The 23-year-old Trump stan may have started out as a social media influencer, but he has quickly become one of the most important people in digital MAGAworld. Pearson helped throw one of the biggest parties during inauguration weekend—sponsored by TikTok—which included some of the trendier names in MAGA politics, such as Riley Gaines and Bryce Hall. Now he’s building his own political-influencer marketing company, leveraging the connections he has made online over the years to support Republican candidates and issues.
paragraphs.zh: 这位 23 岁的特朗普铁杆支持者最初可能是社交媒体意见领袖，但他很快成为数字 MAGA 世界中最重要的人物之一。皮尔森（Pearson）曾协办了总统就职周末规模最大的派对之一——该活动由 TikTok 赞助，邀请了 MAGA 政治圈里一些更“潮流”的人物，例如 Riley Gaines 和 Bryce Hall。如今，他正在打造自己的政治影响者营销公司，利用多年来在网上建立的关系网，为共和党候选人和议题发声并提供支持。

paragraphs.en: Schulz might claim he isn’t political, but the people vying to appear on his show, Flagrant, sure are. The 41-year-old had one of the most memorable interviews with Trump during the campaign when they chatted about universal insurance coverage for IVF. More recently, Schulz has ripped Trump for flip-flopping on the issue. He has also criticized Trump on his refusal to release files from the Jeffrey Epstein case, which he said amounted to “insulting our intelligence.” Now Flagrant is the closest thing to a swing state in the podcast landscape.
paragraphs.zh: 舒尔茨可能会说自己并不涉政，但那些争相上他的节目《Flagrant》的人却显然在玩弄政治。41岁的他在竞选期间曾与特朗普进行过一次最让人难忘的采访，两人在节目里讨论了体外受精（IVF）的全民保险覆盖问题。最近，舒尔茨又因特朗普在这一议题上反复转弯而公开抨击他。他还批评特朗普拒绝公布杰弗里·爱泼斯坦案件文件，称这相当于“侮辱我们的智能”。如今，《Flagrant》已经成了播客领域里最接近“摇摆州”的平台。

paragraphs.en: While Trumpism and its more youth-oriented offshoots are remaking American politics, there’s a far-right renaissance on the other side of the Atlantic too. A new generation of European politicians and influencers are using their clout to continue improving the prospects of parties and policies that were once seen as too extreme to have any chance of success.
paragraphs.zh: 当特朗普主义及其更偏向年轻人的分支在重塑美国政治之际，大西洋彼岸也出现了同向的极右翼复兴。新一代欧洲政治人物和意见领袖正在用自己的影响力，持续为那些曾被认为过于激进、几乎没有胜算的政党与政策争取更好的前景。

paragraphs.en: With over 1 million followers on Instagram, 30-year-old Bardella has leaned into his reputation as a beau gosse—French for a good-looking man—by mixing meticulously edited photos with more casual scenes showing him with his family and glad-handing with voters. Those powerful parasocial relationships make him the perfect 21st-century successor to Marine Le Pen. He knows how to put a TV-ready face on the National Rally, which has gone from a fringe movement in France to the most popular party in the country. With Bardella as its leader, RN has mirrored the GOP and other far-right parties in Europe with its hostility toward supporting Ukraine against the Russian invasion, and continues its long tradition of opposing most forms of immigration. However, sometimes the American variety of fascism is too much, even for him: Bardella canceled an appearance at CPAC—traditionally the biggest conservative confab in the US—after Steve Bannon gave what appeared to be a Nazi salute.
paragraphs.zh: 在Instagram拥有超过100万粉丝的30岁巴德拉，早已将自己“beau gosse”（法语中指英俊男士）的形象经营得炉火纯青：他用精心修饰的照片搭配更随意的生活场景，既展示与家人的时刻，也展示与选民握手致意的画面。这些强烈的“拟社会”关系，使他成为玛丽娜·勒庞在21世纪的理想继承者。他擅长为国民联盟（RN）塑造一种适合电视传播的“公众形象”，也正是这位政党如今从法国边缘运动摇身一变为全国最受欢迎党派的重要原因。巴德拉接手后，RN在对待乌克兰对俄入侵问题上与共和党及欧洲其他极右政党一样，表现出同样强烈的敌意，并且持续延续其长期反对大多数移民形式的传统。然而，有时候美国式法西斯主义对他来说也过于激进：当斯蒂夫·班农向镜头打出疑似纳粹礼后，巴德拉取消了在美国最重要的保守派会议——CPAC上的出席安排。

paragraphs.en: The computer scientist and prolific poster on X, who is in his early thirties, is called Musk’s representative in Italy by local media. Like his role model, Stroppa tries to shape politics through unfiltered posts on social media, whether he’s accusing the Italian government of purportedly drafting “anti-Musk” amendments or calling for the resignation of the interior minister. Given the chance, he’d probably put his home country on the DOGE diet to cut down on government bureaucracy and spending.
paragraphs.zh: 这位30岁出头的计算机科学家兼X（前Twitter）高频发帖者，被当地媒体称为“意大利的马斯克代言人”。与他的榜样一样，斯特罗帕也试图通过社媒上不设滤镜的发帖来塑造政治议程，不论是指控意大利政府正在起草所谓“反马斯克”修正案，还是呼吁内政部长辞职，只要有机会他几乎都会这样做。要是真给他权力，他大概会把自己的祖国推向“DOGE式”紧缩路线，以减少政府官僚体系和财政开支。

paragraphs.en: Source images: Getty, AP Images; Courtesy of Freelancers Union; Mario De Lopez (Melted Solids), Courtesy of Targeted Victory (Amir Hassan)
paragraphs.zh: 图片来源：Getty、AP Images；经 Freelancers Union 提供；Mario De Lopez（Melted Solids）作品，来自 Targeted Victory（Amir Hassan）
